solutions.solution: This question asks you to examine a causal relationship in which you might expect higher prices to result in less demand for a product. Such a relationship has not weakened. What alternative causal explanation could there be for why imports rose? Here, (B) is perhaps the most plausible answer. If (B) is true, then imports would have gone up even if some consumers imports rose only because firms produced items, but the government's trade weakening policies raised tariffs, the resulting higher consumer prices would have if weakening demand for the item might be expected, but no weakening at all.
